subject: mathematics

core_competencies.justification: The chapter is architecturally centred on understanding equality between numerical expressions — section 2.1 introduces comparing expressions via <, >, = and structural reasoning (marble stories), while section 2.2's term-swapping proofs, bracket-removal rules, and distributive property all establish conditions under which two expressions are equal or not.

Section 2.2 explicitly forms and manipulates arithmetic expressions through bracket removal (sign-flip rule, positive-prefix rule) and the distributive property — these are distinct mechanical manipulation operations applied to expressions, forming an independent strand from the equality-reasoning focus of C-2.1.

adjunct_competencies.justification: Throughout section 2.2, students produce and evaluate convincing arguments — the marble-story deductions (section 2.1), the commutativity/associativity proof via term-swapping (Example 6), and the distributive-property proof via repeated-addition (Example 16) are all structured logical arguments, not computations.

The term-identification procedure (rewrite subtraction as addition of inverse, then list terms) and the bracket-removal algorithm are explicit algorithmic decompositions — students follow a programmatic sequence of symbolic operations to transform expressions, directly engaging C-8.1's symbolic representation and ordered-steps thinking.

The Tinker the Terms exercises and E-30 ('create ten different expressions having the same value') invite open-ended creative exploration with no prescribed procedure, requiring students to discover their own expression-construction strategies.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to understand equality between arithmetic expressions by learning to identify terms, apply bracket-removal rules, and use the distributive property to manipulate and compare expressions without computation.
